data.narrative:
Parry Labs is a Series B defense and security company founded by John Parkes and Robert Miller that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis leverages a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses $80M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implying a valuation of $152M. The peer-median method examines 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B). This peer cohort yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $300M to $3.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $129M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $576M. The wide band reflects material dispersion within the peer set—a known characteristic of early-stage defense tech where company-specific differentiation, customer concentration, and classification status drive significant valuation variance. Key uncertainties include Parry Labs' revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and whether its technology profile aligns more closely with lower-valued or higher-valued peers in the cohort. Additional diligence on go-to-market maturity and customer acquisition would refine positioning within this range.
